title: Basic Syntax | Atlas
description: Explore and compare the fundamental syntax building blocks across multiple programming languages.

content.text: How different languages handle memory allocation for data storage. Systems languages like C++ require explicit type definitions, while scripting languages like JavaScript use dynamic typing.
Modern C++ also supports 'auto' for type inference, narrowing the gap between low-level and high-level syntax.
Ensuring data remains unchanged throughout the program's lifecycle.
While 'const' is used in most languages, the underlying mechanics of memory protection vary between compile-time and runtime constants.
Standard mathematical operations and logical comparisons.
Arithmetic syntax is remarkably consistent across C-style languages, making logic migration between environments straightforward.
